factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by higher traffic.

evidence.text: Our passenger revenue increased $246 million, or 3%, compared to the March 2014 quarter on 3.6% higher traffic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing due to higher traffic, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable, with PRASM decreasing slightly but unit revenues growing in certain regions.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue per available seat mile (PRASM) decreased 1.7% and passenger mile yield was flat on 5.0% higher capacity.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: PRASM decreased slightly, but unit revenues grew in certain regions, showing mixed performance.

factors.summary: Capacity is increasing, load factors are slightly declining, and revenue per available seat mile is decreasing.

evidence.text: Load factor was 1.0 point lower than the prior year quarter at 81.7%. Passenger revenue per available seat mile (PRASM) decreased 1.7%.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Capacity is increasing while load factors and PRASM are declining, suggesting operational challenges.

factors.summary: The company is restructuring its fleet by removing older regional aircraft and investing in new models to improve efficiency.

evidence.text: Regional carrier expense. The reduction in regional carrier expense is primarily due to lower fuel cost from a decrease in the cost of fuel per gallon, a contract settlement gain and lower block hours. During the March 2015 quarter, we removed seventeen 50-seat regional aircraft from our fleet as part of our strategy to restructure our domestic fleet.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring the fleet by removing older aircraft and investing in new models improves efficiency.

factors.summary: International regions are facing foreign currency weakness, affecting unit revenues, while domestic demand remains strong.

evidence.text: Revenues related to our international regions decreased 1% year-over-year primarily due to foreign currency weakness versus the dollar and international surcharge declines.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency weakness is affecting international unit revenues despite strong domestic demand.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with lower debt levels and refinanced obligations at lower interest rates.

evidence.text: The decline in interest expense, net results from reduced levels of debt and the refinancing of debt obligations at lower interest rates. Our principal amount of debt and capital leases has declined from $11.4 billion at March 31, 2014 to $9.6 billion at March 31, 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is deleveraging with lower debt and refinancing at lower interest rates, improving financial health.

factors.summary: Demand trends are stable with positive cash flows from operations expected for the remainder of 2015.

evidence.text: We generated positive cash flows from operations of $1.6 billion and $951 million in the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively. We also expect to generate positive cash flows from operations for the remainder of 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive cash flows from operations are expected to continue, indicating strong demand trends.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase, primarily for aircraft purchases and modifications, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditures were $586 million and $614 million in the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively. [...] We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs and A330-300s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A321-200, A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for [...] aircraft modifications.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures for aircraft purchases and modifications signal confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving with positive cash flows and effective use of credit facilities.

evidence.text: We have available $1.9 billion in undrawn revolving lines of credit. [...] We were in compliance with the covenants in our financing agreements at March 31, 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is generating positive cash flows and effectively using credit facilities, indicating strong financial health.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is expected to increase with new aircraft purchases and modifications, enhancing efficiency.

evidence.text: We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft modifications, the majority of which relate to increasing the seat density on our domestic fleet and enhancing the cabins on both our domestic and international fleets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New aircraft purchases and modifications will enhance efficiency, suggesting growth and improvement.

factors.summary: The company is not actively involved in acquisitions or divestitures, as there is no mention of such activities in the provided text.

evidence.text: There is no mention of mergers, acquisitions, or divestitures in the provided text.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No mention of mergers or acquisitions indicates stability but not necessarily positive or negative movement.

factors.summary: Forward guidance is positive with expected continued positive cash flows and capital investments.

evidence.text: We also expect to generate positive cash flows from operations for the remainder of 2015. [...] We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs and A330-300s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A321-200, A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for [...] aircraft modifications.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive cash flow expectations and significant capital investments suggest a favorable outlook.

factors.summary: Management confidence is high, as indicated by increased dividends, share repurchases, and strategic investments in aircraft.

evidence.text: In May 2014, we announced the second phase of capital returns to shareholders. [...] During the March 2015 quarter, we repurchased and retired 9.3 million shares at a cost of $425 million bringing the total under this program to 30.6 million shares at a cost of $1.3 billion. [...] On February 6, 2015, the Board of Directors declared a $0.09 per share dividend for shareholders of record as of February 20, 2015. This dividend was paid in March 2015 and totaled $75 million.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased dividends, share repurchases, and strategic investments demonstrate strong management confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include fuel price volatility and the impact of the company's hedging program, which resulted in margin postings.

evidence.text: The market price for jet fuel is highly volatile, which can impact the comparability of our cash flows from operations from period to period. [...] Our hedge contracts were in a net loss position at March 31, 2015, resulting in $383 million of margin postings to counterparties. Additionally, during the March 2015 quarter, we paid $302 million to early settle contracts that were originally scheduled to expire in the second half of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel price volatility and margin postings due to hedging indicate potential financial instability.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy includes significant capital investments in new aircraft and fleet modifications to enhance efficiency and growth.

evidence.text: We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs and A330-300s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A321-200, A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for [...] aircraft modifications.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant investments in new aircraft and modifications show a clear, growth-oriented strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and their impact on cash flows, as well as the effectiveness of the hedging program.

evidence.text: The market price for jet fuel is highly volatile, which can impact the comparability of our cash flows from operations from period to period. [...] Our future cash flows are impacted by the nature of our derivative contracts and the market price of the commodities underlying our derivative contracts.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to fuel prices and hedging effectiveness present risks but also opportunities depending on market conditions.

factors.summary: A catalyst for future improvement is the expected positive cash flows from operations for the remainder of 2015, supporting further investments and returns to shareholders.

evidence.text: We also expect to generate positive cash flows from operations for the remainder of 2015. [...] We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs and A330-300s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A321-200, A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for [...] aircraft modifications.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The expected positive cash flows from operations will support further investments and returns to shareholders, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Fuel costs are falling, and the company has an unfavorable hedging position due to mark-to-market adjustments.

evidence.text: The impact of unfavorable mark-to-market adjustments recorded on hedges in periods other than the settlement period.

The market price for jet fuel is highly volatile, which can impact the comparability of our cash flows from operations from period to period. [...] Our hedge contracts were in a net loss position at March 31, 2015, resulting in $383 million of margin postings to counterparties. Additionally, during the March 2015 quarter, we paid $302 million to early settle contracts that were originally scheduled to expire in the second half of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel costs are falling, but the company faces unfavorable hedging positions and margin postings, which negatively impacts cash flows.

factors.summary: Fuel costs experienced significant decreases, but also posed volatility and financial strain. The decrease in fuel prices led to lower expenses, yet the company faced challenges with their fuel hedging program, including margin postings and early settlements, which negatively impacted cash flows.

evidence.text: Fuel expense decreased $600 million compared to the prior year quarter due to a 45% decrease in the market price per gallon of fuel.

Fuel expense represented approximately 26% of our total operating expenses for the March 2015 quarter. The market price for jet fuel is highly volatile, which can impact the comparability of our cash flows from operations from period to period.

Our hedge contracts were in a net loss position at March 31, 2015, resulting in $383 million of margin postings to counterparties. Additionally, during the March 2015 quarter, we paid $302 million to early settle contracts that were originally scheduled to expire in the second half of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: While fuel costs decreased, the company faced challenges with its hedging program, leading to margin postings and early settlements that hurt cash flows.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is positive, as operating margins are improving due to lower fuel costs and productivity gains. Additionally, the company is maintaining stable operating margins with positive cash flows and meeting pension plan contributions, supporting its investment in new aircraft.

evidence.text: Our consolidated operating cost per available seat mile (CASM) decreased 8.3% to 14.12 cents from 15.39 cents in the March 2014 quarter.
We contributed $904 million to our qualified defined benefit pension plans during the March 2015 quarter and contributed an additional $296 million in April 2015.

We expect that we will invest approximately $2.8 billion in 2015 primarily for [...] aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs and A330-300s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A321-200, A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for [...] aircraft modifications.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving operating margins due to lower fuel costs and productivity gains, along with stable margins and positive cash flows, support a positive trajectory.

factors.summary: The company is consistently returning capital to shareholders through a combination of buybacks and dividends, which reflects management's confidence in the company's financial health. The evidence shows increasing shareholder returns over time, with both dividend hikes and active share repurchase programs.

evidence.text: We generated $1.6 billion in cash from operating activities, which we used, along with existing cash, to reduce the principal on our debt and capital lease obligations by $260 million, fund capital expenditures of $586 million and return $500 million to shareholders, while maintaining a sufficient liquidity position.

In May 2014, we announced the second phase of capital returns to shareholders. [...] During the March 2015 quarter, we repurchased and retired 9.3 million shares at a cost of $425 million bringing the total under this program to 30.6 million shares at a cost of $1.3 billion.
On February 6, 2015, the Board of Directors declared a $0.09 per share dividend for shareholders of record as of February 20, 2015. This dividend was paid in March 2015 and totaled $75 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is consistently returning capital to shareholders through buybacks and dividends, reflecting confidence in its financial health.
